title: Choose an isolation model deliberately
description: Pick silo, pool, or bridge isolation per workload from the security, cost, and noisy-neighbor trade-offs rather than defaulting to shared everything.
body: Use when designing how tenants share or separate infrastructure. Do not put every tenant in one shared pool without weighing isolation needs. Actions: assess data-sensitivity and blast-radius requirements; choose silo (dedicated resources) for the strictest tenants, pool (shared with logical separation) for density, or a bridge mix; document which tenants get which. Validation signals include a model whose isolation guarantees match each tenant's requirements and a documented mapping. Risk boundary: an under-isolated model for sensitive tenants is hard to reverse once data lands, so decide with security and escalate high-sensitivity tenants for review.
